submissions.app_golden_code:
import numpy as np
N=128
w=np.exp(2j*np.pi/N)
Q=np.array([[w**(j*k) for k in range(N)] for j in range(N)],complex)/np.sqrt(N)
golden=Q.conj().T
submissions.construction_method: Direct construction of QFT_7 = (1/sqrt(128)) * exp(2pi*i*j*k/128), then conjugate transpose. Big-endian indexing, no bit-reversal.

submissions.app_golden_code:
import numpy as np
U=np.zeros((64,64),complex)
for s in range(64):
    c=(s>>5)&1; y=s&31
    y2=(2*y%21) if(c==1 and y<21) else y
    U[(c<<5)|y2,s]=1
golden=U
submissions.construction_method: Controlled permutation: for each 6-bit input s, split c=MSB and 5-bit work y. When c=0: identity. When c=1: y->2y mod 21 for y<21, fixed for y>=21. Output index = (c<<5)|y2.

submissions.app_golden_code:
import numpy as np
U=np.zeros((64,64),complex)
for s in range(64):
    c=(s>>5)&1; y=s&31
    y2=(4*y%21) if(c==1 and y<21) else y
    U[(c<<5)|y2,s]=1
golden=U
submissions.construction_method: Same structure as cmul2 but with multiplier 4. y->4y mod 21 for y<21, fixed for y>=21.

submissions.app_golden_code:
import numpy as np
U=np.zeros((64,64),complex)
for s in range(64):
    c=(s>>5)&1; y=s&31
    y2=(16*y%21) if(c==1 and y<21) else y
    U[(c<<5)|y2,s]=1
golden=U
submissions.construction_method: Same structure with multiplier 16. Note 16=2^4 and ord(2 mod 21)=6, so 16y mod 21 gives genuine modular arithmetic (not bit-rotation).
